fills: Tabel 4.5 headline robustness — does the lagged-Kalman lead survive power?
predictor: lagged-Kalman (P(correct,t)=kalman_mastery_after[t-1]) — the SAME predictor as the headline (lagged-Kalman, 0.6051 tie-aware); isolates predictor from power vs Task 7's rejected 3-learner matched predictor
verdict: HEADLINE LEAD SURVIVES POWER (tie-aware AUC). HCIE lagged-Kalman overall AUC leads BKT at EVERY power level: +0.0088 (n=10), +0.0062 (n=40), +0.0125 (n=76). The lead is POSITIVE throughout and is LARGEST and STATISTICALLY SIGNIFICANT at n=76 (95% CI [+0.0017, +0.0226], per-user cluster bootstrap), unlike n=10 (CI crosses 0). IMPORTANT honesty correction: under the tie-aware convention the margin is NOT monotonic -- it dips at n=40 -- so the claim is 'positive at all power levels, largest and significant at full power', NOT 'monotonic growth' (the earlier monotonic +0.0245->0.0252->0.0324 used the optimistic tie convention). Per-window: BKT still leads the cold-start cells (<=10/<=20) at every level -- the documented Simpson pattern -- while HCIE leads pooled-overall (and edges <=5 at n=76). Framing stays 'competitive'.
framing: AUC = competitive, not superior. Overall lead positive at all power, significant at n=76; per-window deep/BKT leads are a Simpson/cold-start pattern, not a loss. Tie-aware AUC (Rekomendasi A).
per_window_note: On the SAME lagged-Kalman headline predictor, tie-aware: BKT leads the per-window cold-start cells (<=10/<=20) at n=40 and n=76 -- the documented Simpson pattern -- while HCIE leads pooled-overall and edges <=5 at n=76 (+0.0369).

auc_tie_convention.supersedes: the optimistic-tie power sweep (+0.0245->+0.0251->+0.0324). HCIE has ~26.5% rows tied at the cold-start prior; tie-aware is the honest convention (Rekomendasi A).
auc_tie_convention.tie_aware_significance_n76: HCIE-BKT = +0.0125, 95% CI [+0.0017, +0.0226] (per-user cluster bootstrap, B=3000) -> SIGNIFICANT at n=76; at n=10 (+0.0088) the CI crosses 0 (not significant).
